metadata.evaluation_name: ATMS Hallucination Meter Iter-1 Rigorous Evaluation

cot.note: Baselines lack per-instance confidence; ARC = flat error rate

linc.note: Baselines lack per-instance confidence; ARC = flat error rate

problog.note: Baselines lack per-instance confidence; ARC = flat error rate

l1_degradation_theorem.statement: Let epsilon_L1 be the per-atom L1 grounding error rate (probability any given atom is incorrectly grounded), with grounding errors independent across atoms. For an empty-environment derivation requiring a chain of k atoms: P(derivation fully correct) >= (1 - epsilon_L1)^k. Therefore empty-environment precision >= (1 - epsilon_L1)^k_max, where k_max is the maximum chain length in the derivation.
l1_degradation_theorem.jensen_bound: E[precision_EE] >= exp(-epsilon_L1 * k_bar) for mean depth k_bar
l1_degradation_theorem.note: With observed epsilon_L1=0.0 (all instances L1-verified), the bound collapses to 1.0. The bound is non-trivial for epsilon_L1 > 0: e.g., epsilon_L1=0.04 (Path-of-Thoughts 95.9% triplet accuracy), k=3 hops → lower bound = (0.96)^3 = 0.885.

Document: In Smith v. Jones (2021), the court ruled that Smith, who is the landlord of the disputed property, had failed to provide adequate notice before eviction. The tenant, Jones, had lived at the property for five years. The judge, Honorable Williams, ordered compensation of $5,000.
